raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"\"Check this\" plausibly demands verifying that the causal chain from experiment design → 12-condition WER results → reproducibility-bounds claim → deployment recommendations is internally coherent and free of inferential leaps.","R1.3":"The artifact asserts specific WER figures (3.1%, 8.7%) and a named-entity substitution finding; a generic \"check\" naturally encompasses confirming those quantitative claims are consistent with the described methodology and scope.","R1.6":"\"Check this\" is routinely used as a proofreading delegation, making sentence- and paragraph-level polish a plausible load-bearing interpretation alongside the substantive checks."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on verifying the logical and evidential integrity of the claims and reported data (R1.2/R1.3), rather than reviewing and improving the writing quality (R1.6)?"}

rationale.R1.2: "Check this" plausibly demands verifying that the causal chain from experiment design → 12-condition WER results → reproducibility-bounds claim → deployment recommendations is internally coherent and free of inferential leaps.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact asserts specific WER figures (3.1%, 8.7%) and a named-entity substitution finding; a generic "check" naturally encompasses confirming those quantitative claims are consistent with the described methodology and scope.
rationale.R1.6: "Check this" is routinely used as a proofreading delegation, making sentence- and paragraph-level polish a plausible load-bearing interpretation alongside the substantive checks.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on verifying the logical and evidential integrity of the claims and reported data (R1.2/R1.3), rather than reviewing and improving the writing quality (R1.6)?
